intake.user_request: populate-coderef graph validator chokes on minified public/*.js bundles (e.g. public/workbox-4754cb34.js), reporting duplicate node identities (#n:1, #r:1, #i:1 — single-letter minified identifiers colliding). This aborts populate, which blocks complexity/summary.json generation, which blocks workorder creation. Fix: exclude generated public/*.js build artifacts from scan/populate scope. Verify against gridiron-franchise project.

classification.plan_type_rationale: Rule 6 fallthrough: focused exclusion-pattern fix in 2 co-located files (ignore-rules.ts + scanner.ts). No architectural change, no multi-layer pipeline impact. Light plan sufficient.

in_scope: Add minified JS bundle patterns to DEFAULT_PIPELINE_IGNORE_PATTERNS in src/pipeline/ignore-rules.ts
Add same patterns to DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS in src/scanner/scanner.ts
Verify gridiron-franchise populate exits 0 after fix
Update docs/CLI.md comment if --help output changes (unlikely)
out_of_scope: Changes to the graph validator logic itself
Changes to any other CLI commands or pipeline stages
Adding new CLI flags for per-project exclusion (already exists via .coderefignore)
Refactoring the ignore-rules or scanner architecture

constraints: Fix must not exclude hand-authored JS in public/ if any — use specific minified-bundle patterns, not public/**
Exclusion must be convention-based (filename patterns), not path-based exclusion of all public/
Verify gridiron-franchise populate exits 0 after fix
Both ignore-rules.ts and scanner.ts DEFAULT patterns must be updated for consistency
